file.content:
import {
  Box,
  Text,
  ListTextIcon,
  Button,
  InputBox,
  Icon,
  BackDropBottom,
} from '@semparar/design-system-app';
import React, { FC } from 'react';
import BasicScreen from '../../atomic/templates/BasicScreen';
import { InvoiceCashbackViewProps } from './models';
import {
  formatCurrencyBR,
  getFormattedPoints,
  getParsedCurrency,
  getParsePoints,
} from '../../utils/functions';
import { Skeleton } from '../../components/TransactionsListFactory/styles';

const InvoiceCashbackPointsView: FC<InvoiceCashbackViewProps> = ({
  handleGoBack,
  handleContinue,
  points,
  formattedPrice,
  handleChangeReais,
  handleChangePoints,
  handleUseAll,
  pointsValue,
  reaisValue,
  convertionValue,
  pointsError,
  reaisError,
  items,
  pointsQuantity,
  handleModalizeContinue,
  onOpenDefault,
  modalizeRef1,
  handleSelectEvent,
  height,
  width,
  isLoadingParameterization,
}) => {
  return (
    <BasicScreen
      hasScroll
      hasHeader
      bgColor="BRAND_COMPLEMENT_03"
      leftIconOnPress={handleGoBack}
      title="Cashback na fatura"
      hasRightIcon
      rightIcon="icon-help"
      rightIconOnPress={onOpenDefault}
      errorButtonOnPress={() => {}}
      isLoading={false}
    >
      <Box ml="smaller" mr="smaller" flex={1} justify="flex-start">
        <Text
          color="INTENSE_COLOR_05"
          testID="invoice-cashback-main-title"
          type="headingMDL"
          mt="smaller"
        >
          Defina quanto cashback quer na sua fatura
        </Text>

        <Box direction="row" mb="smaller" mt="nano" align="center">
          <Text type="paragraphMD" mr="quark">
            Pontos disponíveis:
          </Text>
          <Text type="paragraphXXXL" mr="quark">
            {getFormattedPoints(points || 0)}
          </Text>
          <Text type="paragraphMD">({formattedPrice})</Text>
        </Box>

        <InputBox
          label="Valor em pontos"
          placeholder="0"
          showLabel
          keyboardType="number-pad"
          value={pointsValue}
          onPressIn={() => handleSelectEvent('campo_pontos')}
          onChangeText={handleChangePoints}
          error={pointsError}
          textError="Você precisa informar um valor menor."
          textRight="Usar todos"
          onPressTextRight={handleUseAll}
        />

        <Box direction="row" mb="xxs" mt="xxs" align="center">
          <Box height="1px" bgColor="INTENSE_COLOR_02" width="45%" />
          <Icon
            name="icon-switch-vertical"
            size="xxs"
            color="INTENSE_COLOR_03"
            mr="nano"
            ml="nano"
          />
          <Box height="1px" bgColor="INTENSE_COLOR_02" width="45%" />
        </Box>

        <InputBox
          label="Valor em reais"
          placeholder="R$0,00"
          showLabel
          keyboardType="decimal-pad"
          onPressIn={() => handleSelectEvent('campo_valor')}
          value={reaisValue}
          onChangeText={handleChangeReais}
          error={reaisError}
          textError="Você precisa informar um valor menor."
        />
        {isLoadingParameterization && (
          <Box direction="row" mb="xxs" mt="xxs" align="center">
            <Skeleton width={width - 40} height={60} />
          </Box>
        )}
        {!isLoadingParameterization && (
          <Box direction="row" mb="xxs" mt="xxs" align="center">
            <Text type="paragraphMD">
              Cada {pointsQuantity || '0'} pontos valem {convertionValue || formatCurrencyBR(0)} de
              cashback na fatura.
            </Text>
          </Box>
        )}
      </Box>
      <Box ml="smaller" mr="smaller">
        <Button
          disabled={
            pointsValue === '' ||
            reaisValue === '' ||
            pointsValue === '0' ||
            reaisValue === '0' ||
            pointsError ||
            reaisError
          }
          text="Continuar"
          mt="large"
          mb="small"
          onPress={() => {
            const ptsSel = getParsePoints(pointsValue);
            const reaisSel = getParsedCurrency(reaisValue);
            handleContinue({ pointsSelected: ptsSel, reaisSelected: reaisSel });
          }}
          testID="invoice-cashback-button"
        />
      </Box>
      <BackDropBottom modalHeight={height * 0.53} modalizeRef={modalizeRef1}>
        <Text type="subtitleXXL" mb="smaller" mt="smaller" align="center">
          Use seus pontos para pagar menos na fatura
        </Text>

        <ListTextIcon items={items} ml="nano" mr="nano" type="list" mb="smaller" />

        <Button text="Entendi" onPress={handleModalizeContinue} />
      </BackDropBottom>
    </BasicScreen>
  );
};

export default InvoiceCashbackPointsView;

Perfect. Now I have all the information I need to provide a comprehensive review. Let me create the verdict:

## MIGRATION REVIEW: InvoiceCashbackPoints models/index.ts

**VERDICT: PASS** ✓

---

### Detailed Analysis

#### 1. **Redux-related imports removed correctly?**
**PASS** ✓
- `mapDispatchToProps` removed (was never directly used in models)
- `mapStateToProps` removed (was never directly used in models)
- `ScreenProps` type removed (Redux HOC wrapper pattern eliminated)
- No Redux-related types remain in the models file

#### 2. **NativeStackScreenProps imported correctly?**
**PASS** ✓
- Imported from correct source: `@react-navigation/native-stack`
- Line 4: `import { NativeStackScreenProps } from '@react-navigation/native-stack';`
- This is the standard React Navigation pattern for typed screen props

#### 3. **PropsFintechCardRoutes imported from the right location?**
**PASS** ✓
- Imported from correct path: `../../../routes/Models`
- Verified in `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram/src/routes/Models/index.ts`
- Type correctly defines `InvoiceCashbackPoints` route with proper params structure (lines 31-35 of routes/Models)
- Route params include: `conversionFactor`, `productCode`, `campaingId`, and `OptionalAnalyticsProps`

#### 4. **InvoiceCashbackProps type correctly updated?**
**PASS** ✓
```typescript
export interface InvoiceCashbackProps
  extends NativeStackScreenProps<PropsFintechCardRoutes, 'InvoiceCashbackPoints'> {}
```
- Properly extends React Navigation's native stack screen props
- Correctly parameterized with `PropsFintechCardRoutes` and route name `'InvoiceCashbackPoints'`
- This gives full type safety for `route` and `navigation` props
- Verified usage in index.tsx (line 9, 20, 52-54) shows proper typing

#### 5. **Other types unchanged?**
**PASS** ✓
- **InvoiceCashbackViewProps** (lines 7-32): Unchanged, properly typed for view component
- **CashbackInvoiceItem** (lines 37-44): Unchanged, maintains proper shape with `TextIconType` and icon properties
- Both types correctly support the functional component patterns used in view.tsx

### Integration Verification

✓ The index.tsx component correctly uses the migrated types:
  - Line 9: Imports `InvoiceCashbackProps` from models
  - Line 10: Imports `PropsRootStack` for navigation typing
  - Line 20: Component typed with `FC<InvoiceCashbackProps>` with `route` destructuring
  - Line 52: Navigation properly typed with `NavigationProp<PropsRootStack>`
  - Lines 54, 83-89: Route params accessed safely with proper typing

✓ No Redux imports remain in any InvoiceCashbackPoints files
✓ All navigation uses React Navigation patterns consistently
✓ Type-safe route params enable full IDE autocomplete

---

**Summary**: The migration cleanly removes Redux dependencies and adopts React Navigation's native typing system. All imports are correct, the type hierarchy is sound, and integration with both the route definitions and component usage is seamless.
